reason: The live status command keeps quota, token-history, Linux process sampling, responsive terminal rendering, and focused regressions together; capped until resource collection or rendering needs independent ownership.
Runtime launch command routing, provider selection, fixed harness resolution, and session/runtime entry planning remain capped after live goal recovery moved to its focused module.
The local control-plane HTTP server, redacted JSON endpoints, provider/model catalog assembly, and dashboard tests remain capped while endpoint handlers are split from server orchestration.
Login flow TUI prompts, provider handling, and persisted profile setup remain capped while provider-specific flow ownership is extracted.
Copilot profile import, direct runtime API auth, model catalog refresh, legacy token fallback, and bounded HTTP response reads remain capped while auth refresh is split from profile import ownership.
Kiro profile import now discovers the native CLI model catalog with an ACP fallback while preserving private credential staging; capped until catalog discovery splits from command orchestration.

Data-plane authorization, dispatch, reconciliation, and provider runtime signals remain capped after governance context, policy, routing, session persistence, and audit phases moved to focused modules.
Kiro ACP process lifecycle, streaming sequence state, conversation persistence, and runtime metadata orchestration remain capped after pure protocol shaping moved to provider-core.

Local rewrite cross-provider regression coverage remains capped after harness-mode cases and governance-failure behavior moved to focused sibling modules; provider-specific cases continue to split from the shared harness.
DeepSeek request rewrite regression coverage remains capped while compatibility cases are split by feature family.

Gateway usage regressions now cover atomic budget admission, header-first virtual-key rejection, bounded persistence queues, and request-id cleanup; capped until admission and persistence fixtures are split.
Provider bridge regressions include the fail-closed route-normalization matrix alongside compact routing, accounting, and continuation parity; capped until tests split by provider.
Runtime launch command tests remain capped after secure credential fixtures and post-exit session maintenance moved into focused sibling modules.
Runtime launch run-command strategy regression coverage remains capped after live goal recovery cases moved to their focused module.
App-server protocol fixture, schema drift, replay, lifecycle, affinity, redaction, and stdio broker regressions remain capped while the shared harness is split by protocol concern.
WebSocket precommit hold regressions now include oversized response-created quota retry boundaries; capped until sideband and terminal suites split.
HTTP continuation, tool-output, and compact regression coverage remains capped while compact-specific fixtures are split by route.

Compact admission and quota/transport rotation regression coverage now carries the two explicit immutable RuntimeConfig fixture fields and remains capped while fixture setup is split from route-specific assertions.
Profile export regression fixtures include bounded v1/v2 migration and resource-exhaustion cases; capped until provider-specific scenarios split from shared helpers.
Profile import regression fixtures now cover bounded journal and credential recovery cases; capped until provider-specific import cases are split from shared helpers.
Provider-aware runtime and gateway argument parsing now includes typed harness selection and explicitly rejects normalized triple-slash URLs; the tightly coupled CLI surface remains capped.
DeepSeek bridge request, response, stream, tool-call, usage, and error conformance regressions remain colocated with the compatibility bridge until split by fixture family.
Gemini bridge request, response, streaming, tool-call, multimodal, usage, and error conformance regressions remain colocated until split by fixture family.
Cross-provider conformance v1 fixtures verify capability, transform status, stream termination, usage, error, and tool-call contracts across every supported provider.
Profile export envelope and import-journal regression coverage now includes bounded artifact cases; capped until fixtures split by envelope and journal domain.
Session file I/O now lives in a focused sibling; remaining report resolution and resume repair stay capped until repair helpers split.
Large-file regressions now live in a focused sibling; remaining state-db, unreadable-file, and metadata-synthesis fixtures stay capped by scenario.
Gateway admin CRUD and typed provider/harness catalog compatibility coverage remain capped while tenant, key, SCIM, and audit fixtures are split by resource.
Application boundary characterization now verifies that governed inspection and policy plans cross data-plane admission; capped while security, accounting, and policy cases move to focused modules.
Authorization boundary characterization remains capped while data-plane, control-plane, and break-glass cases are split by surface.
Config boundary characterization remains capped while refresh, invalidation, publication, and secret-reference cases are split.
Control-plane boundary characterization remains capped while tenant, identity, key, and policy flows are split into focused modules.
Accounting domain characterization remains capped while reservation, reconciliation, and idempotency cases are split.
Audit domain characterization remains capped while query, retention, digest, and export tests are split.
Observability boundary characterization is capped while metric, trace, log, SLO, and redaction tests are split.
Runtime policy compatibility coverage includes typed gateway harness validation and remains capped while versioned policy, cache, and gateway settings tests are split.
Storage boundary characterization is capped while accounting, migration, idempotency, and readiness tests are split.
PostgreSQL SQL-plan contracts are capped until migrations, reservation accounting, idempotency, and RLS plans are split.
PostgreSQL storage characterization is capped while migration, tenant isolation, and accounting tests are split.

SQLite compatibility SQL-plan contracts export versioned enterprise-governance migrations including session revision and revocation metadata; capped until local accounting, idempotency, and readiness plans are split.
SQLite storage characterization is capped while migration, tenant isolation, and accounting tests are split.
Shared Codex home preparation now covers corrupt transcript metadata recovery; capped until history, transcript, and attachment fixtures split by domain.
External provider catalog generation now imports Kiro model metadata alongside Copilot while preserving exact secret/config validation; capped until provider-specific catalog loading splits out.
Gemini request translation remains capped while multimodal and tool request fixtures are split by protocol concern.

Gateway SQLite, PostgreSQL, and Redis compatibility migration handling now threads one fail-closed TLS policy through blocking opens and migrations; capped while backend-specific connections are split.
Gateway key-store compatibility, exact decoding, backend persistence, and explicit local PostgreSQL TLS fixtures remain capped until backend implementations are split.
Gateway admin auth coverage proves background-only OIDC discovery/JWKS refresh; canonical OIDC/SCIM evidence coverage moved to a focused nested fixture, and the remaining legacy matrix stays capped.
Gateway durable state and cross-proxy PostgreSQL/Redis admission coverage includes explicit local PostgreSQL plaintext setup and remains capped while backend scenarios are split.
Enterprise control-plane composition, storage setup, publication, observability, and the thin route-isolated serve dispatch remain capped while command handlers are split.
Enterprise OTLP log export and redaction integration remain capped while transport and serialization are split.
Enterprise binary characterization remains capped while control-plane, gateway, migration, and observability fixtures are split.

Gateway launch composes governed snapshot authorities, durable audit/session workers, immutable harness diagnostics, bounded refresh, and SIEM workers; capped until authority bootstrap moves to a focused module.
The four-artifact maker-checker lifecycle and payload-free execution-approval HTTP flow remain colocated to preserve tenant, idempotency, ETag, audit, activation, and rollback semantics.
The bounded session bank keeps admission, durable command handling, revision pinning, revocation epochs, bank fail-closed refresh, and monotonic classification invariants together.
Lifecycle HTTP coverage exercises four governed artifact kinds plus execution-approval quorum, denial audit, idempotent replay, and content-free responses through shared fixtures.
Typed deployment configuration validates governance mode floors, anonymous compatibility, and enterprise topology contracts.
Governed routing security coverage keeps hard filters, runtime health/quota/load signals, deterministic score, eligible fallback, affinity, revocation, and bounds in one matrix.
Runtime policy types carry fixed gateway harness selection plus bounded PIP selectors, governance authority, lifecycle, session, audit, and retention configuration in one typed schema.
Runtime-policy validation coverage keeps governance mode, PIP selector, unsupported workload identity, and dependency failure matrices with the gateway policy suite.
The PostgreSQL governance facade retains tenant-scoped repository methods and transaction ordering after revision, approval, session, and audit/outbox helpers moved to focused backend modules.
The SQLite governance facade retains repository APIs and explicit transaction ordering after revision, approval, session, and audit/outbox helpers moved to focused backend modules.
SQLite governance integration coverage proves lifecycle, approval idempotency and denial audit, session revisions/revocation, audit integrity, bounded export, and SIEM outbox behavior.
Runtime governance compiles built-in and tenant policy snapshots plus bounded PIP selectors and compiler regressions; capped until DTO translation moves to a focused module.
Gateway virtual-key admission now carries trusted team, project, and user scope into the governed PIP while preserving key mutation and accounting behavior.
The runtime provider registry compiles approved descriptors, projected heterogeneous adapters, bounded dynamic signals, and focused conformance tests; capped until adapter projection splits out.
The pure governance PDP keeps bounded PIP attributes, typed conditions and obligations, conflict analysis, deterministic evaluation, and redacted contracts together.

The async gateway server now owns transport peer trust, bounded forwarded-chain parsing, Host/Origin/CSRF enforcement, body limits, upgrades, and graceful drain; capped until edge transport splits out.
Gateway server integration coverage keeps canonical targets, streaming limits, overload, trusted proxy, forwarding privacy, Host/Origin/CSRF, and expected-host cases in one harness.
The governance storage contract keeps public revision, approval, session, audit, and SIEM DTOs together while pure codecs, validation, and integrity helpers live in focused child modules.
